Today.title: Why is Indias Asset and Wealth Management industry expected to nearly double by 2030?
Today.content:
Summary:

With assets under management expected to increase from Rs. 84.81 lakh crore in 2024 to Rs. 160.19 lakh crore by 2030, the asset and wealth management sector in India is expected to nearly treble. Between 2024 and 2030, it is predicted to increase at a CAGR of 11.6%, surpassing AsiaPacific's 6.8% growth. Institutional capital, retail involvement, regulatory changes, digital infrastructure, and GIFT City's rise to prominence as a global financial center are the main drivers of growth. These elements direct household savings into long-term investments and strengthen capital markets. India's financialisation is bolstered by the country's 80% bank account penetration rate and growing UPI digital payments. With increasing demat accounts, SIP inflows, and institutional funds from EPFO and insurance, UPI processes Rs. 235.58 lakh crore yearly.

Source: IBEF

Today.title: How are India and Seychelles strengthening their economic ties?
Today.content:
Summary:

Through trade, investment, connectivity, renewable energy, and collaboration on the blue economy, India and the Seychelles are fortifying their economic ties. Bilateral trade increased by about 15% from the previous year to Rs. 703 crore (US$84.88 million) in 2023 - 2024. India's imports totalled Rs. 72 crore, while its exports totalled Rs. 631 crore. Between April 2024 and February 2025, commerce totalled Rs. 617 crore, of which Rs. 580 crore came from exports and Rs. 36 crore from imports. Improved air connection, such as IndiGo's Mumbai - Mahé flights that began in 2025, has helped Indian companies including Bank of Baroda, Bharti Airtel, Tata Motors, and Ashok Leyland grow in the Seychelles. Solar energy, blue economy initiatives, and financial transparency are all areas of cooperation that build linkages to sustainable development.

Source: IBEF

Today.title: How is India accelerating export growth through the Export Promotion Mission and industry collaboration?
Today.content:
Summary:

In order to assess the Export Promotion Mission (EPM) and boost India's export growth, Union Commerce and Industry Minister Mr. Piyush Goyal presided over a meeting with Export Promotion Councils (EPCs) and industry associations. A concerted plan to increase exports, investigate new markets, and bolster EPC contributions was the main topic of discussion. The Minister encouraged EPCs to collaborate closely with the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) by utilising free trade agreements and providing quantifiable proposals. In order to guarantee quicker, time-bound decisions and minimise delays, the government also instituted a set schedule for important committees. Mr. Rajesh Agarwal, the commerce secretary, emphasised the use of support mechanisms such as logistics assistance, export factoring, and credit guarantees. In order to increase competitiveness, the summit also supported GI-tagged products, District Export Hubs, and ecommerce exports.

Source: IBEF

Today.title: Why is India emerging as the most receptive market for agentic AI in the Asia Pacific region?
Today.content:
Summary:

According to Adobe's 2026 AI and Digital Trends Report, India is becoming the Asia Pacific region's most open market for agentic AI, with over 60% of consumers interested in building a personal AI agent. With 55% of respondents wanting to engage with brand AI agents and 58% at ease with agent-to-agent conversation, the study demonstrates a high degree of openness toward the usage of AI. Approximately 61% of people would consent to AI speaking with human agents on their behalf. India exhibits a high level of confidence when it comes to embracing new technologies. 65% of people use AI for personalised suggestions, 60% for customer service, and 62% are open to AI shopping assistants, demonstrating how AI is permeating daily life. Despite productivity benefits, obstacles include data problems, talent gaps, ROI uncertainties, and infrastructure limitations.

Source: IBEF

Today.title: Why is Indias real estate sector attracting rising institutional investment in 2026?
Today.content:
Summary:

Despite inflationary pressures, economic volatility, and geopolitical unpredictability, India's real estate market continued to draw significant institutional investment in the first half of 2026. JLL India reports that from January to June 2026, institutional investment increased 23% year over year to Rs. 40,801 crore (US$4.33 billion), with 54 transactions - the greatest number of half-year deals ever. This comes after a record investment of Rs. 98,942 crore in 2025. With the largest amount ever recorded - Rs. 26,384 crore and 64% of total volumes - domestic investors predominated, countering weaker foreign inflows with the help of private equity funds and REITs. Driven by GCC development and rental yields, the office market led investments with Rs. 21,673 crore across 17 agreements. 34% of capital flows were drawn to Bengaluru and Chennai combined. Investments in 2026 could total between Rs. 80,096 and Rs. 84,807 crore.

Source: IBEF

Today.title: How is India boosting mango exports through sea shipment to Singapore?
Today.content:
Summary:

The first commercial marine shipment of premium Banganapalle mangoes from India to Singapore was successfully assisted by APEDA, a division of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, working with ICAR-CISH. On June 11, 2026, Osum Food Solutions LLP exported the 5 MT shipment, which arrived in Singapore on June 24, 2026. The shipment complied with Singapore's quality and phytosanitary criteria after being sourced from GAP-certified orchards in Andhra Pradesh and processed at a packhouse recognised by APEDA in Karnataka. It emphasises sustainable and economical logistics for exports of horticulture products. The export showed excellent post-harvest and cold-chain management, preserving fruit quality while in transit. Farmers profited greatly, receiving roughly Rs. 50 per kg as opposed to local rates of Rs. 25 - 26 per kg, almost doubling returns and enhancing India's export potential.

Source: IBEF

26-06-2026.title: How are Indias new-age consumer brands driving rapid growth in the FMCG sector?
26-06-2026.content:
Summary:

According to Bain & Company and DSG Consumer Partners, India's new-age consumer brands are expanding more than three times faster than the FMCG sector. In FY25, their revenue increased from Rs. 18,942 crore to Rs. 71,033 crore. Digital commerce, fast commerce, and innovation in snacking, cosmetics, personal care, jewellery, home goods, and wearables are the main drivers of growth. These brands outperform larger marketplaces despite having a share of less than 2% because they cater to changing consumer demands. The industries with the biggest increases are jewellery and cosmetics, which are growing 6.5 times and 6 times faster than the market. Protein snacks, healthier drinks, beauty goods with scientific backing, and ergonomic items are how they foster customer loyalty. Scaling is still difficult, with only 22% surpassing Rs. 500 crore and very few surpassing Rs. 100 crore.

Source: IBEF

26-06-2026.title: How is India strengthening its export ecosystem and boosting global trade competitiveness?
26-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal met with industry associations and Export Promotion Councils to discuss ways to boost India's export environment, increase its market share, and boost its competitiveness internationally. Supporting MSMEs, utilising free trade agreements, fostering innovation, developing deep-tech capabilities, enhancing export facilitation, and expanding involvement in global value chains were the main topics of discussion. Industry representatives highlighted initiatives to improve Brand India internationally by sharing sector-wise export performance and progress on key performance indicators. In May 2026, India's exports reached a six-month high of Rs. 4.26 lakh crore, up 18% year over year. There was a trade deficit of Rs. 5.32 lakh crore during April and May of FY27, with exports reaching Rs. 8.38 lakh crore and imports reaching Rs. 13.70 lakh crore.

Source: IBEF

26-06-2026.title: How are India and the US strengthening bilateral trade ties?
26-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In the face of shifting global trade dynamics, India and the US have advanced talks to improve bilateral trade and expand economic cooperation. In New Delhi, US Trade Representative Ambassador Jamieson Greer met with Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal to discuss current trade talks and discuss potential new areas of cooperation. The proposed bilateral trade agreement, which addresses market access, digital trade, supply chain resilience, lowering non-tariff barriers, and collaboration in key areas, was the main topic of discussion. In order to sustain longterm growth and investment, both sides reiterated their commitment to increasing trade, fostering innovation, and creating reliable supply chains. With robust flows in both directions, India-US goods trade reached Rs. 14.09 lakh crore in 2025. The collaboration seeks to increase investment, market access, and economic resilience.

Source: IBEF

26-06-2026.title: How is digitalisation transforming India’s steel sector?
26-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In order to increase production, efficiency, and global competitiveness, India's steel industry is being urged to embrace new technologies. Union Steel Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy stated at Chintan Shivir 2026 on Digitalisation in the Steel Sector that intelligent, technologically advanced manufacturing systems - rather than just production capacity—will determine the industry's future. By increasing productivity, streamlining processes, and facilitating data-driven decision-making, technologies like artificial intelligence, machine learning, the industrial internet of things, digital twins, robotics, and advanced data analytics are revolutionising the steel industry. With the help of infrastructural development, Make in India, renewable energy, and urbanisation, the government hopes to reach 300 million tonnes of steel capacity by 2030 and 400 million tonnes by 2035. India's steel competitiveness will be strengthened and difficulties will be addressed with the aid of digitalisation.

Source: IBEF

26-06-2026.title: How is Indias trade partnership with Latin America expected to grow by 2030?
26-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Due to closer economic links, trade between India and Latin America is expected to treble from Rs. 4.74 lakh crore (US$50 billion) to Rs. 9.47 lakh crore (US$100 billion) by 2030. Alberto Antonio Guani Amarilla, Uruguay's ambassador to India and GRULAC coordinator, stated at the LAC FIRST conference that Latin America will continue to be a vital ally as India advances toward being a developed economy. He outlined significant chances for boosting commerce and investment as well as cooperation. Additionally, he praised India's choice to establish an embassy in Uruguay, describing it as a strengthening of bilateral ties. The ambassador called for the India-Mercosur trade pact to be expanded from 450 to around 3,000 items. Minerals, agro-industry, pharmaceuticals, and renewable energy are important industries.

Source: IBEF

26-06-2026.title: How is GEMS Education expanding its presence in Indias education sector?
26-06-2026.content:
Summary:

The Varkey Group's GEMS Education India intends to increase its footprint in India's education industry by investing Rs. 284.13 crore (US$30 million) over the course of the next three to five years. More than 30 GEMS-operated schools, a network of more than 1,000 GEMS Partner Schools, and a School of Education for teacher preparation, leadership development, and research are all part of the plan. Over three million students will gain from the program, and over 200,000 teachers will have chances. The first five of the 21 school projects that GEMS India has identified will launch in various states in 2026. It is anticipated that the network will employ 10,000 teachers and serve more than 100,000 pupils. Additionally, the business intends to establish an Alternative Investment Fund to aid in the development of school infrastructure.

Source: IBEF

25-06-2026.title: How Can Indias Tourism and Hospitality Industry Unlock Its Potential to Create 100 Million New Jobs by 2047?
25-06-2026.content:
Summary:

By 2047, it is anticipated that India's tourist and hospitality sector would have grown significantly, adding about 100 million new direct jobs. The Federation of Associations in Indian Tourism and Hospitality (FAITH) estimates that the industry may boost the economy by Rs. 284.13 lakh crore (US$3 trillion) per year, with foreign exchange profits potentially reaching Rs. 42.62 lakh crore (US$450 billion). Puneet Chhatwal stressed the importance of using more effective international marketing and promotions to draw in more foreign visitors. India can use its rich cultural, spiritual, and natural history to achieve sustainable year-round tourist growth with the support of government-led destination development and improved infrastructure, such as airports, highways, and rail links.

25-06-2026.title: How Is PM-KISAN Transforming Indian Agriculture?
25-06-2026.content:
Summary:

The PM-KISAN program keeps improving Indian farmers' financial inclusion and income stability. 9.44 crore farmers, including 2.18 crore women, received Rs. 18,880 crore on June 20, 2026, as part of the 23rd installment. Through DBT, the program has disbursed more than Rs. 4.46 lakh crore since 2019, giving qualified families Rs. 6,000 a year for agricultural costs. For ongoing assistance, the Union Budget 2026–2027 set aside Rs. 60,000 crore. Through Aadhaar authentication, e-KYC, online registration, and mobile services, PM-KISAN further advances digital government. Together with programs like AgriStack and Kisan-eMitra, it has strengthened inclusive rural development through technology-driven governance, boosted productivity, improved access to agricultural services, and supported sustainable growth and modernised India's agricultural ecosystem for future resilience.

Source: IBEF

25-06-2026.title: How Is India’s Video Entertainment Industry Adapting to Changing Consumer Preferences and Digital Trends?
25-06-2026.content:
Summary:

The video entertainment industry in India is changing into a hybrid market where viewers have a say in the production, distribution, and monetisation of content on various platforms. To adapt to shifting customer needs, entertainment organisations are implementing blended revenue models that combine subscriptions, advertising, commerce, and transactions. As a result, a multi-format ecosystem with premium content, creator-led videos, and micro-dramas is now available on mobile devices, connected TVs, and televisions. While increasing the variety of viewing options and monetisation tactics, major platforms such as JioStar, Prime Video, and YouTube are merging technology, commerce, and content. While connected TVs, IPTV, and broadband services continue to gain popularity, mobile devices continue to be the primary screen. In order to remain competitive in the face of divided consumer attention, entertainment organisations are adopting flexible business models and diverse distribution tactics.

Source: IBEF

25-06-2026.title: How Is India Planning to Achieve US$ 100 Billion in Textile and Apparel Exports by 2030?
25-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In order to help India reach Rs. 9.47 lakh crore (US$100 billion) in textile and apparel exports by 2030, the Ministry of Textiles has started a statewide effort. In order to establish strategies centred on technological textiles, man-made fibres, infrastructure, logistics, technology, and market access, the two-day summit brings together states, industry leaders, and exporters. State and district export action plans have been created after talks with approximately 200 districts, 36 states, and Union Territories. The program promotes district-led export expansion and cooperative federalism. Giriraj Singh, the minister of textiles, highlighted branding, value addition, diversification, sustainability, and skill. The National Textile Export Roadmap 2030 will direct initiatives to improve global competitiveness and fortify the textile value chain.

25-06-2026.title: How Will Indias Commercial Office REITs Expand and Sustain Growth by FY28?
25-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Due to asset additions and new REIT listings, it is anticipated that listed commercial office REITs in India will increase their leasable space to 190–195 million square feet by FY28. Strong credit profiles will be maintained by increasing rental revenue, portfolio diversification, and regulated leverage, according to Crisil Ratings. Growth through the purchase of operational assets will guarantee steady cash flows and lower construction risks. It is anticipated that demand from global capability centers, BFSI firms, and flexible workspace operators will maintain occupancy at 92– 93%. Operating margins of about 70% will be supported by contract-based rental increases, and debt levels are probably going to be reasonable at loan-to-value ratios of 26–28%. AI-driven workplace shifts, the slowing economy, and more REIT listings, however, continue to be major obstacles and indicators for the industry going forward.

Source: IBEF

25-06-2026.title: How Will Adani Group’s Airport Investments Strengthen India’s Aviation and Regional Connectivity?
25-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Adani Group plans to invest Rs. 90,000 crore to Rs. 1 lakh crore across its airport portfolio over the next five years to strengthen India’s aviation infrastructure and connectivity. The investment supports airport expansion, including commercial operations at Mundra Airport with Star Air services linking Kutch to Mumbai and Goa. The group also plans to participate in bidding for 11 new airports. Enhanced connectivity will promote trade, tourism and regional development, with future routes including Ahmedabad. Mundra Airport can accommodate larger aircraft, enabling future growth in passenger traffic. The expansion aligns with the UDAN initiative and is expected to boost India’s aviation ecosystem, infrastructure development and economic growth through sustainable investments and improved regional air transport networks.

24-06-2026.title: How Is Indias Green Housing Market Becoming More Sustainable and Transparent?
24-06-2026.content:
Summary:

India's green housing market is expanding as buyers and developers place a higher priority on affordability, efficiency, and sustainability. According to a Square Yards analysis, green homes can reduce energy expenses by 20–30% and water consumption by 30–50%. Despite the fact that there are more than 19,700 green projects, consumers are unable to compare their sustainability qualities using a uniform system. Square Yards and The Habitat Enterprise developed the Square Yards Green Living Index (SYGLI) to increase transparency. The framework evaluates projects across energy, water, environmental quality, resilience, and mobility factors utilising RERA data, developer disclosures, and geospatial intelligence. Green housing and trustworthy sustainability standards will promote healthier, climate-resilient urban living for the nation's long-term net-zero transition and future, because construction accounts for approximately one-third of India's emissions.

24-06-2026.title: How Has India Become the World’s Leading Ship Recycling Nation in 2025?
24-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In 2025, India became the world's top ship recycling country, surpassing its Maritime India Vision 2030 targets. With a volume of 2.99 million GT, the nation's contribution of worldwide recycling increased to 35.4%. 115 yards were able to comply with Hong Kong Convention requirements because to financial assistance, government reforms, and the Recycling of Ships Act of 2019. Recycling and local construction are further encouraged by the Ship-breaking Credit Note Scheme. India wants to increase Alang's capacity to nine million LDT. India is well-positioned to handle 500–600 ships a year, bolstering its position as a sustainable global maritime hub and supporting its long-term objective of maritime leadership and growth. Over 16,000 vessels are anticipated to be recycled globally in the next ten years.

Source: IBEF

24-06-2026.title: How Will Kavach Deployment Strengthen Railway Safety and Efficiency in India?
24-06-2026.content:
Summary:

At a cost of Rs. 27 crore, Indian Railways has authorised the installation of Kavach, an indigenous Automatic Train Protection system, along 631 route km of the East Coast Railway. As part of Kavach's statewide development through the use of an LTE-based communication network, the project will cover six important railway sections. Through continuous train monitoring and automated braking, the technology enhances railway safety by preventing signal passing at hazard, speeding, and crashes. Additionally, Kavach will improve operating effectiveness, dependability, and timeliness, particularly in inclement weather like heavy fog. In addition to helping Indian Railways modernise its infrastructure, the program will help create a national railway network that is safer, quicker, more effective, and robust for future transportation demands.

24-06-2026.title: How Is the Aadhaar App Transforming Digital Identity Services and Citizen Convenience in India?
24-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In just five months, the Aadhaar app has been downloaded over 31 million times, demonstrating the growing confidence in digital identity services. It was created by UIDAI and allows users to securely share identity information, download e-Aadhaar, schedule appointments at Aadhaar Seva Kendra, and update mobile numbers and addresses. The software has completed 850,000 address adjustments and nearly 4 million mobile updates. Biometric lock/unlock, facial authentication, authentication history, and QR-based information exchange are some of its features. Hotel checkins, hospital admissions, visitor management, event entry, and gig worker authentication are all supported by the app. By offering safe, easily accessible, and convenient citizen-centric identification services that improve daily governance and digital service delivery across the country, this Android and iOS app fortifies India's digital public infrastructure.

24-06-2026.title: How Is India Emerging as a Global Technology Powerhouse Through Digital Innovation and Advanced Technologies?
24-06-2026.content:
Summary:

With investments in digital infrastructure, artificial intelligence, semiconductors, quantum technology, and sophisticated computing, India is becoming a global leader in technology. 99.9% of districts now have 5G services, while the Digital India initiative has increased internet access and optical fibre networks. Technological self-reliance is being strengthened by significant projects including the National Quantum Mission, IndiaAI Mission, and Semicon India Programme. Over 2,100 Global Capability Centers, extensive AI usage by startups, and 38 supercomputers under the National Supercomputing Mission comprise India's innovation ecosystem. The nation's biotechnology industry has grown to be worth US$190 billion, and its Global Innovation Index ranking rose to 38th in 2025, demonstrating its development as a leader in global innovation and a technological innovator.

Source: IBEF

24-06-2026.title: How Will Maruti Suzukis Advanced Manufacturing Labs Strengthen Skill Development and Indias Automotive Workforce?
24-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Maruti Suzuki India has signed an MoU with Gujarat’s Directorate of Employment and Training to establish five Advanced Manufacturing Labs at ITIs across the state. These labs in Palanpur, Bhavnagar, Surendranagar, Godhra and Dahod will provide hands-on training in modern automotive manufacturing and industry-standard practices. The initiative aims to bridge the gap between technical education and workplace requirements, improving student employability and supporting Gujarat’s industrial growth. Maruti Suzuki currently supports 31 ITIs and 18 Advanced Manufacturing Labs nationwide in manufacturing-related trades. With the addition of five new labs, its total AMLs will rise to 23 across seven states and Union Territories. The initiative will strengthen industry-academia collaboration and create a future-ready workforce for India's manufacturing ambitions.

Source: IBEF

23-06-2026.title: How is the Asian Development Bank planning to expand its private sector investments in India in 2026?
23-06-2026.content:
Summary:

India continues to be the largest market for private sector activities of the Asian Development Bank, which intends to provide development-aligned projects with direct financing of around US$1 billion (Rs 9,436 crore) in 2026. It intends to keep up the momentum from 2025, when it committed more than US$4 billion in sovereign finance and US$1 billion in private sector investment, while raising comparable external funds to achieve almost US$2 billion in total private flows. Renewable energy, green hydrogen, electric vehicles, sustainable infrastructure, green data centers, aiding the climate transition, energy security, and long-term competitiveness are important investment sectors. ADB aims to mobilise private finance and bolster confidence in India's growth and green transition, while India also receives help for renewable energy, urban development, logistics, transportation, and skills development. optimistic outlook.

Source: IBEF

23-06-2026.title: How are Indias exports performing amid global challenges?
23-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Despite tariff pressures and worldwide concerns, India's product exports demonstrated remarkable resilience. According to Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal, exports increased by almost 15% year over year from April 1 and June 14, 2026, demonstrating the power of commerce in the face of global problems. Exports reached a six-month high of Rs. 4.26 lakh crore (US$45.2 billion) in May 2026, an 18% increase over the previous year. Exports jumped 16.09% to Rs. 8.39 lakh crore in April and May of 2026, while imports increased 15.14% to Rs. 13.71 lakh crore, resulting in a Rs. 5.33 lakh crore trade imbalance. India is still optimistic that its exports would hit $1 trillion in FY27. The goal of trade negotiations with the US on June 23–24, 2026, is to enhance market access and lessen tariff concerns in order to promote sustained export growth.

Source: IBEF

23-06-2026.title: How has Indias defence production and exports grown since 2014 and what new manufacturing facility is being developed?
23-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Strong industrial growth was demonstrated by India's domestic defence output, which increased from Rs. 46,000 crore (US$7.54 billion) in 2014 to over Rs. 1,78,000 crore (US$20.42 billion) in FY25. Due to the growing demand for Indian-made equipment worldwide, defence exports rose from Rs. 1,000 crore prior to 2014 to around Rs. 40,000 crore. military Minister Rajnath Singh emphasised independence in military manufacture by laying the groundwork for a 10,000-ton aluminium extrusion press at Yantra India Limited in Nagpur. The plant would serve railways, ISRO, and DRDO while producing aluminium alloy extrusions for the defence, aerospace, and key industries. It boosts the growth of indigenous industry while lowering imports.

23-06-2026.title: How are India and the UK strengthening cooperation in AI, innovation, and trade under the CETA agreement?
23-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In addition to potential under the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), India and the UK have talked about strengthening cooperation in artificial intelligence, new technologies, research, and innovation. In order to promote shared economic growth and closer bilateral ties, discussions between UK Permanent Secretary Emran Mian and India's High Commissioner to the UK P. Kumaran concentrated on bolstering technology collaborations, innovation ecosystems, and cooperative research. The talks focused on how to use CETA to open up new commercial, research, and innovation opportunities in high-growth industries including artificial intelligence and cutting-edge technologies. The agreement is anticipated to promote MSMEs, startups, and long-term innovation-led development while expanding trade, investment, and market access ahead of the implementation of CETA and the Double Contribution Convention (DCC) on July 15, 2026.

Source: IBEF

23-06-2026.title: How is Reliance Industries planning to build India’s AI ecosystem through its new artificial intelligence roadmap and platforms?
23-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In order to establish India as a global leader in AI-driven innovation and to construct the nation's independent AI backbone, Reliance Industries has unveiled an ambitious artificial intelligence plan. To serve cutting-edge applications across industries, the company intends to create one of the biggest AI computing platforms in the world. By making significant investments in cutting-edge technology and facilitating broader access to AI tools for companies, developers, and consumers, this effort aims to boost India's digital capabilities. Reliance also unveiled AI-powered services, such as an AI-driven home platform to incorporate smart services into homes and the Jio AI Call Agent for intelligent voice conversations. Together, these initiatives seek to boost digital transformation, increase the application of AI across industries, and bolster India's innovation environment and technological independence.

23-06-2026.title: What are Aleta Planet’s plans to expand its cross-border payment business and launch Visa debit card programmes in India?
23-06-2026.content:
Summary:

As part of its regional expansion plan in cross-border and multi-currency payments, Singaporebased fintech company Aleta Planet is entering the Indian market. In order to expedite the introduction of card programs for financial institutions, the business intends to collaborate with licensed Indian entities to develop plug-and-play infrastructure for Visa debit card issuance, payments, and compliance. Because of its strong economic ties to Singapore and the Middle East, as well as its quickly expanding digital payments market, Aleta views India as a significant possibility. Additionally, a senior director has been appointed to oversee partnerships with an emphasis on India. By partnering with Visa and Sanrio to introduce themed debit and prepaid cards with characters like Hello Kitty, the company hopes to duplicate its Singapore model. On June 22, 2026, about 9,000 cards will be made available.

Source: IBEF

22-06-2026.title: How Will Indias C&I Energy Storage Market Reach 22 - 31 GWh by 2032?
22-06-2026.content:
Summary:

IESA projects that the C&I energy storage market in India will grow from less than 1 GWh in 2025 to 22–31 GWh by 2032. Rising power prices, the use of renewable energy, dependability requirements, and decarbonisation objectives are what propel growth. Energy optimisation, resilience, and cost savings are replacing backup applications in the market. Capacity could reach 22–23 GWh under the BAU scenario, but quick adoption could raise it to 31 GWh. Lead-acid, lithium-ion, sodium-ion, vanadium-redox flow, and pumped hydro systems are examples of storage technologies. Energy storage is a crucial component of India's clean energy transition, with significant potential for long-term sustainable industrial growth ahead, thanks to improving economics, expanding renewable integration, and regulatory backing.

Source: IBEF

22-06-2026.title: How Is the Indian Diaspora Strengthening India-France Relations and Innovation Cooperation?
22-06-2026.content:
Summary:

During his visit to Paris following the G7 Summit, Prime Minister Narendra Modi commended the Indian diaspora in France for improving India-France relations. He emphasised their function in fostering tighter interpersonal relationships, economic involvement, and cultural bonds. Participating in VivaTech 2026, which showcases India's digital economy, startups, and developing technologies, is another aspect of the tour. In the areas of military, marine security, renewable energy, artificial intelligence, innovation, and sophisticated manufacturing, France and India are still expanding their collaboration. Bilateral ties have been further strengthened by initiatives like the India-France Innovation Roadmap 2030 and collaborations in research, technology, and skilling. The visit highlights how crucial innovation-driven cooperation and the diaspora will be to furthering the strategic alliance between both countries in the next years.

22-06-2026.title: How Has Indias Pharmaceutical Sector Transformed Over the Past 12 Years to Become a Global Healthcare Leader?
22-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Over the past 12 years, India's pharmaceutical industry has undergone substantial change, resulting in increased access to healthcare, more reasonably priced medications, and indigenous manufacture. The nation is now the third-largest pharmaceutical manufacturer in the world by volume and a significant provider of generic medications to more than 200 nations and territories. Through more than 15,000 Janaushadhi Kendras, government programs including the PLI Scheme, Bulk Drug Parks, and PMBJP have increased manufacturing and increased access to reasonably priced medications. Complex generics and biosimilars are now more capable thanks to increased investments in R&D, biotechnology, vaccines, and infrastructure. India's status as the world's pharmacy was strengthened by its involvement in the COVID-19 epidemic. In the upcoming years, exports, employment, and long-term sector growth will be supported by a sustained emphasis on innovation, reforms, and self-reliance.

Source: IBEF

22-06-2026.title: How Will India Achieve Its FY27 Target of 26,474 km of Rural Roads and Strengthen LastMile Connectivity?
22-06-2026.content:
Summary:

The Centre has targeted construction of 26,474 km of rural roads in FY27 with an allocation of Rs. 18,907 crore under PMGSY and related schemes. A review meeting led by Rural Development Secretary Rohit Kansal assessed progress under PMGSY and RCPLWEA, emphasising faster completion of unconnected habitations, particularly in remote and tribal regions. The Government prioritised connectivity for Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups through improved DPR preparation, timely land acquisition and stronger coordination among agencies. Focus was also placed on completing projects in Maoist-affected areas, maintaining infrastructure quality and ensuring effective road upkeep. Wider adoption of the e-MARG platform will enhance transparency, monitoring, payment tracking and efficiency, supporting reliable all-weather rural connectivity across India.

Source: IBEF

22-06-2026.title: How Will the BRICS MSME Forum Boost Global MSME Cooperation?
22-06-2026.content:
Summary:

The First BRICS MSME Forum and the Third SME Working Group Meeting were held in Agra by the Ministry of MSME with the topic "Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability." In order to fortify MSME ecosystems, advance sustainability, and strengthen international cooperation, the event brought together policymakers, business executives, and entrepreneurs from BRICS and partner countries. With more than 8.6 crore businesses, the MSME sector in India accounts for 31.1% of GDP, 35.4% of manufacturing output, and 48.58% of exports. The program included talks and exhibits on sustainable growth, technology adoption, and finance. In order to build competitive, future-ready MSMEs, delegates shared best practices and policy experiences. This strengthened BRICS cooperation and revealed new avenues for innovation, entrepreneurship, and global market integration.

22-06-2026.title: How Is Nykaa Planning to Achieve Rs. 47,635 Crore GMV by FY30 Through Expansion and AI?
22-06-2026.content:
Summary:

By FY30, Nykaa hopes to reach GMV of Rs. 47,635 crore (US$5 billion), propelled by growing consumer spending and expansion in the beauty and fashion sectors. The company anticipates a two-to three-fold increase in sales, a four-to five-fold increase in EBITDA, and an improvement in margins to low-to-mid teens. It intends to greatly increase its beauty and fashion industries and build its retail network to more than 600 locations. Through more than 40 GenAI projects, such as automated advertising, personalised suggestions, and AI-powered customer service, Nykaa is also speeding up digital transformation. Long-term growth in India's growing beauty and lifestyle retail sector is anticipated to be supported by its omnichannel strategy, rise of owned brands, and increased fashion profitability.

19-06-2026.title: How has Indias agricultural export basket diversified and strengthened its global position over the past decade?
19-06-2026.content:
Summary:

According to APEDA Chairman Abhishek Dev, India's agricultural export basket has grown dramatically over the last ten years, from roughly 280 products to nearly 500 products. Last year, exports reached approximately Rs 4.61 lakh crore (US$53 billion), maintaining India's position among the top ten agricultural exporters in the world despite competition from the US, Brazil, China, and the EU. The growth is a result of initiatives to increase market access, diversify exports, and boost the agriculture sector's competitiveness in international markets. Value-added and indemand goods including pulses, cocoa products, pineapples, grapes, and pomegranates are now among India's agricultural exports. These goods are sold in numerous foreign markets, demonstrating the increasing popularity of Indian agricultural products around the world and promoting farmers' livelihoods and sustainable rural development.

19-06-2026.title: How is Indias solar-powered agriculture model shaping sustainable farming globally?
19-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Particularly in Africa, India's agricultural revolution driven by renewable energy is becoming recognised globally as a paradigm for sustainable development. Through programs like PMKUSUM and the International Solar Alliance, it emphasises how solar-powered farming can improve rural livelihoods, farmer incomes, and food security. India demonstrates how renewable energy may fill up irrigation shortages, especially in Sub-Saharan Africa, where irrigation coverage is far lower than in India. Better crop output, less reliance on diesel, and cheaper irrigation are all supported by the model. Millions of solar pumps have been made possible by PM-KUSUM, and more are anticipated by 2030. The goal of Mission 300 is to grow solar agriculture for power. India is establishing itself as a global leader in clean energy integration and sustainable agriculture thanks to its quick expansion of solar and renewable energy capacity.

19-06-2026.title: How is India becoming a global electronics manufacturing hub?
19-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Driven by increased production, exports, and regulatory backing, India is quickly solidifying its place as a global center for electronics manufacturing. Speaking during the opening of the Jabil Pune facility, Ashwini Vaishnaw stated that India is becoming a popular manufacturing site and the sixth-largest exporter of electronics. India seeks to become second-largest exporter through investment in manufacturing and supply chains. With two factories currently under construction and two more planned, the government is increasing semiconductor capacity. Pune is developing precision manufacturing. With a robust infrastructure and around 60% of data center capacity, Maharashtra is an important hub. Large-scale initiatives like Wadhwan Port and the MumbaiAhmedabad Bullet Train will improve commerce, growth, and logistics. India's place in the global electronics manufacturing and export ecosystem growth is strengthened by this.

19-06-2026.title: What are the current growth trends, demand outlook, and pricing dynamics shaping India’s steel industry?
19-06-2026.content:
Summary:

With domestic demand reaching 9% year over year in May 2026 and 8.7% in FY26 thus far, India's steel industry is exhibiting strong development momentum. According to Kotak Institutional Equities, since demand growth continues to outperform capacity expansions, capacity utilisation is predicted to remain around 90% over the medium term. After four years of double-digit growth, steel demand increased by 7.6% in FY26, demonstrating the robustness of the market. While imports increased to 0.7 million tonnes, exports increased by 30% year over year to 0.5 million tonnes. Urbanisation, manufacturing growth, and infrastructure development are important factors that influence consumption. Price trends are still inconsistent, with HRC remaining comparatively constant and rebar falling. Higher trade prices could counteract rising coal and iron ore costs and help further improvements in margins.

19-06-2026.title: How is generative AI transforming healthcare adoption, patient experience, and expectations in India?
19-06-2026.content:
Summary:

According to a Bain & Company analysis, generative AI (GenAI) is increasingly being used in India to comprehend medical issues, get ready for consultations, and traverse healthcare systems, making it one of the Asia-Pacific region's most AI-ready healthcare markets. Approximately 78% of Indian consumers rely on GenAI for healthcare navigation, 73% for scheduling medical appointments, and 78% for understanding diagnosis and treatment options. According to the report, 88% of customers want healthcare to be more convenient, and nearly 80% want doctors to be reachable via phone or text. A single point of contact for care coordination is preferred by about 93% of respondents. High expenditures (43%), lengthy wait times (42%), and appointment delays (30%) are major issues that indicate a significant desire for improved, AI-enabled healthcare systems.

19-06-2026.title: How is global investment accelerating India’s data centre expansion and digital infrastructure growth?
19-06-2026.content:
Summary:

CtrlS Datacenters and the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPP Investments) have teamed to increase India's digital infrastructure with a commitment of up to Rs 6,710.21 crore (US$710 million). Strong international investor trust in India's rapidly expanding data center industry, which is fuelled by increased demand for cloud computing, hyperscale facilities, and AI-ready infrastructure, is reflected in this move. To purchase an 8.2% share, CPP Investments will invest Rs 3,945.81 crore, making CtrlS worth roughly Rs 44,325.19 crore. Additionally, the two partners will establish a joint venture, of which CPP would own 48% and CtrlS 52%, to build hyperscale data center campuses throughout India. Cloud services, businesses, and AI applications will all benefit from the investment. It is anticipated that India's data center capacity would expand quickly, bolstering its digital economy and drawing sustained foreign capital inflows.

18-06-2026.title: How did Indias battery energy storage capacity grow so rapidly in Q1 2026?
18-06-2026.content:
Summary:

India's energy storage industry grew significantly in the first quarter of 2026 due to a significant increase in battery installations brought on by the need to integrate renewable energy. Mercom India Research reports that between January and March 2026, 4.6 GWh were added, a 941% increase over the 442.7 MWh from the previous quarter. By March 2026, this increased installed capacity to 5.9 GWh, enhancing grid stability and absorption of renewable energy. Gujarat had the biggest independent battery pipeline, but Rajasthan had the most cumulative capacity. Additionally, India issued 18 GW of energy storage contracts and 7.2 GW of pumped storage during the quarter, indicating significant potential momentum. These results demonstrate India's long-term effort toward a resilient low-carbon electricity infrastructure and its rapid growth.

18-06-2026.title: What were the key outcomes of Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s “historic and productive” visit to Slovakia?
18-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In an effort to improve bilateral ties, Prime Minister Narendra Modi wrapped up a historic and fruitful visit to Slovakia. Strong ties were evident as Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico bid him farewell. Slovakia's highest civilian award, the Order of White Double Cross (1st Class), was given to President Peter Pellegrini for the 33rd time internationally. A path for further interaction was created and cooperation was reviewed. To promote safe and lawful labour mobility, a number of Memorandums of Understanding pertaining to education, research, talent mobility, technology, culture, and labour migration were inked. In order to increase trade and investment in the automotive, electronics, and advanced technology industries, they decided to fortify the Joint Economic Committee. Following a visit that emphasised strategic and economic collaboration, he departed for France to attend the G7 Summit.

18-06-2026.title: How are India - France relations strengthening through innovation, technology collaboration, and investment partnerships?
18-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal claims that under Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Emmanuel Macron, India-France relations are rapidly improving in the fields of technology, innovation, strategy, and economics. In recent years, cooperation has grown to include areas driven by innovation, economics, and strategy. France has been urged to become more involved in India's growth narrative, emphasising chances for long-term collaborations, investment, and codevelopment. India is being positioned as a worldwide center for manufacturing and innovation, creating more opportunities for cooperation. Goyal visited Sophia Antipolis, Europe's largest tech park with more than 2,600 businesses that demonstrate research-driven growth, while in France. While Bharat Innovates 2026 in Paris showcased 120 entrepreneurs and 350 investors, indicating growing global confidence, meetings in Nice concentrated on fortifying relationships.

18-06-2026.title: Is India preparing additional policy measures to boost capital inflows amid global economic uncertainties?
18-06-2026.content:
Summary:

India is prepared to withstand global economic concerns, according to Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman, who stated that the country is willing to adopt further policy measures to increase capital inflows if necessary. She pointed out that while the government and RBI have already implemented policies to boost investor confidence and draw in foreign capital, these are just the first stages. Depending on how things change, more steps might be taken. Maintaining consistent investment inflows to promote growth and financial stability continues to be the major priority. Currency fluctuations, unstable pricing for fertiliser and crude oil, and erratic monsoon patterns are some of the external forces now affecting the economy. In order to boost investor confidence and preserve general stability and economic resilience, the government is keeping a careful eye on these risks while carrying out changes like tax breaks and market-friendly laws.

Source: IBEF

18-06-2026.title: How did Bharat Innovates 2026 Day 2 accelerate DeepTech investment, global collaboration, and startup funding opportunities?
18-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In order to accelerate DeepTech innovation and fortify international technological collaborations, Bharat Innovates 2026 Day 2 brought together global investors, entrepreneurs, researchers, politicians, and industry leaders. The Government of India and the Ministry of Education hosted the event, which featured an innovation exhibition with Indian companies and academic institutions showcasing cutting-edge technologies in biotechnology, semiconductors, artificial intelligence, healthcare, energy, mobility, and space. Jalaj Dani, the keynote speaker, emphasised strong industry collaboration and matching innovation with market need. Advanced manufacturing, defence supply chains, and bio-innovation were all discussed on the panel. Over 40 of the more than 80 firms that pitched to more than 50 international investors from ten different countries received follow-ups. With over 1,350 B2B meetings and investments of Rs. 2,360.95 crore, the summit strengthened India's expanding worldwide DeepTech leadership and ecosystem.

18-06-2026.title: Will Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles achieve industry-leading growth in FY27 through its EV push and multi-powertrain strategy?
18-06-2026.content:
Summary:

With a robust product pipeline, a broad multi-powertrain strategy, and steady expansion in the electric vehicle market, Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles hopes to achieve industry-leading growth in FY27. Despite the uncertainty surrounding the world economy, the firm anticipates that these elements will help it outperform the passenger vehicle market as a whole. Its array of ICE, CNG, hybrid, and EV models is intended to draw in a variety of clientele and solidify its position in important markets. Expansion is anticipated to be further fuelled by rising demand and ongoing product innovation. Additionally, the corporation views its collaboration with Jaguar Land Rover as a significant benefit that enhances size, efficiency, and competitiveness. With an ongoing emphasis on electrification, innovation, and portfolio development, Tata Motors hopes to bolster its leadership and accomplish long-term, sustainable growth.

17-06-2026.title: What is the FY27 outlook for Indias office market growth?
17-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Despite an 8% decline in H1 due to low Grade A supply, India's commercial office market is predicted to continue growing steadily into FY27, with gross leasing likely to rise 12 - 14% year over year to 85 - 90 million square feet. FY26 leasing is estimated at 79 - 80 million square feet, up roughly 10%. Growth is fuelled by the GCC's expansion, which accounts for 35 - 45% of demand, and the growing use of flexible workspaces. By 2026, India is expected to surpass 100 million square feet, making it the biggest flex market in Asia-Pacific. With premium assets maintaining their strong prices, supply is slowly increasing, rentals are solidifying, and vacancies are predicted to remain steady.

Source: IBEF

17-06-2026.title: How is the government improving ease of living for the middle class in India?
17-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Prime Minister Narendra Modi highlighted the government's ongoing commitment to making life easier for India's middle class, describing it as essential to the country's progress. He pointed out that the middle class has contributed significantly to the development of the country and that fulfilling its ambitions continues to be a top priority for governance. Better infrastructure, more opportunities, and effective public services have all been key components of policies aimed at enhancing quality of life. Significant expenditures in housing, digital systems, highways, railroads, and urban expansion have improved living conditions and connectedness. In addition to increasing access to healthcare, education, and cleaner cities, reforms seek to lessen daily pressures. Convenience and service accessibility have expanded thanks to digital governance and better civic infrastructure. These initiatives promote confidence and inclusive growth.

17-06-2026.title: How are India and Slovakia strengthening their economic partnership through trade and investment in 2025?
17-06-2026.content:
Summary:

With significant increases in trade and investment, Slovakia and India are deepening their economic links. After surpassing US$1 billion in 2024, bilateral trade increased by almost 28% to a record Rs. 15,305.43 crore (US$1.81 billion) in 2025. Between 2021 and 2025, India's exports to Slovakia increased significantly, with automotive parts, engineering goods, machinery, electronics, and medicines leading the way. Slovakia's imports also rose, mostly in the form of machinery and passenger cars, indicating a more integrated supply chain. Major Indian enterprises working in Slovakia, such as TCS and other engineering and transportation companies, as well as the Tata Group's Jaguar Land Rover plant in Nitra, are increasing investment collaboration. Slovak businesses are bolstering industrial cooperation in India by investing in manufacturing, energy, railroads, and technology.

Source: IBEF

17-06-2026.title: How is Hero FinCorp using Salesforce Agentforce to improve loan processing and digital lending efficiency across India?
17-06-2026.content:
Summary:

By optimising loan processing with Agentforce and associated technologies like Data 360 and MuleSoft, Hero FinCorp has worked with Salesforce to improve its digital lending processes. Important processes like document extraction, application validation, identification checks (such as PAN and Aadhaar verification), credit evaluations, e-signature initiation, and loan disbursement are all automated by the system. With 92% of applications handled through automated workflows and 77% fewer NIGO situations at the sales stage, this has led to a 72% decrease in turnaround time for two-wheeler loans. Over 6,461 dealers in India are supported by the solution, which increases productivity and lowers manual error rates. Hero FinCorp is extending AI-driven solutions across loan products to enhance scalability, customer experience, and responsible lending for millions of clients nationwide, building on its Salesforce base.

Source: IBEF

17-06-2026.title: How Is the India - France Partnership Driving Innovation, Technology, and Investment Towards a Viksit Bharat 2047?
17-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In order to support India's Viksit Bharat 2047 goal, Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal emphasised that India-France cooperation is a significant force for innovation, technology, and investment. He discussed ways to increase trade, technology, and emerging sector cooperation with government officials, business executives, investors, and innovators during the India-France Year of Innovation engagements in Nice. The India-France Innovation Roadmap 2030 seeks to improve cooperation in academia, research, critical technologies, and sustainable development. It is anticipated that the collaboration will improve investments, technical development, and economic resilience. In addition, Prime Minister Narendra Modi and French President Emmanuel Macron talked about strengthening bilateral ties and generating international economic possibilities for both nations while extending strategic cooperation in defence, space, security, counterterrorism, technology, and innovation.

17-06-2026.title: How Is India Strengthening Long-Term Economic Growth?
17-06-2026.content:
Summary:

India is well-positioned for long-term economic growth because of robust domestic demand and economic resilience, according to Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman. She emphasised government and RBI actions, such as FAR regulations and investor tax incentives, to draw in foreign investment and enhance access to international capital markets at the Mindmine Summit 2026. In order to assist the expansion of investment, she pointed out that public sector banks and initiatives might raise money abroad. India's sizable market promotes stability in the face of global difficulties like supply chain problems, geopolitical conflicts, oil price volatility, and climate hazards. In order to guarantee inclusive and sustainable economic development while preserving readiness against weather-related and other developing hazards, the government will continue to implement reforms, fortify institutions, and support creative initiatives.

16-06-2026.title: How Has Indias Startup Ecosystem Grown in the Past Decade?
16-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Over the past ten years, India's startup ecosystem has grown dramatically, producing about 25 lakh jobs and rising from roughly 350 recognised businesses in 2016 to over 2.3 lakh today. Dr. Jitendra Singh claims that this expansion is a reflection of India's rise to prominence as a major global startup hub, bolstered by programs like Startup India, incubation centers, digital infrastructure, and policies that prioritise innovation. IT, biotechnology, healthcare, agriculture, fintech, deep technology, and manufacturing are among the industries where startups are spearheading innovation. Entrepreneurship and investment have been bolstered by easier access to capital, mentorship, and incubator support. Through increased creativity, productivity, and global competitiveness, startups are predicted to contribute to economic growth, technical advancement, job creation, and India's Viksit Bharat 2047 goal.

16-06-2026.title: How Are Indias Youth Driving Innovation, Entrepreneurship and National Development?
16-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Prime Minister Narendra Modi emphasised the government's focus on youth-led growth, pointing out that programs like the Atal Innovation Mission, Startup India, Digital India, and Skill India have encouraged young people to pursue entrepreneurship and innovation. With young people spearheading business expansion in both urban and rural areas, these initiatives have helped establish India as a top startup destination. He underlined how young Indians are making substantial contributions to fields like science, technology, industry, space, semiconductors, and drones, bolstering India's scientific and economic advancement. The Prime Minister also acknowledged the accomplishments of young athletes, who have benefited from better sports facilities and chances. Viksit Bharat 2047 and national development objectives are anticipated to be advanced by sustained investment in skills, innovation, entrepreneurship, and sports.

16-06-2026.title: How Will the India - France Partnership Boost Innovation and Trade?
16-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In order to improve collaboration under their Special Global Strategic Partnership, France and India have announced 13 outcomes. The India-France Innovation Roadmap 2030, a Joint Artificial Intelligence Working Group, a National Center of Excellence for Skilling in Aeronautics at NSTI Kanpur, the growth of UPI in France, and assistance for ten more Indian entrepreneurs at Station F are some of the major projects. A High-Level Mechanism to double bilateral trade within five years and an Economic Security Dialogue centred on supply chains and crucial industries were also agreed upon by both nations. Digital sciences, digital health, railroads, academic exchanges, and space research are further areas of collaboration. Innovation, commerce, technology cooperation, strategic relationships, and long-term economic growth are all anticipated to increase as a result of these actions.

16-06-2026.title: How Will Varya Strengthen Indias Indigenous AI Video Generation Capabilities?
16-06-2026.content:
Summary:

With assistance from the IndiaAI Mission, Avataar has introduced Varya, an indigenous AI-powered video creation model that advances India's attempts to create domestic AI capabilities. Varya aspires to make video creation accessible for educators, MSMEs, content creators, and public service providers by producing culturally relevant content that reflects India's languages, regions, festivals, and communities. The IndiaAI Mission's government-sponsored AI compute infrastructure was used to create the model. Varya employs a sophisticated distillation technique that cuts the process from 50 steps to four while preserving quality in order to solve the high cost of AI video generation. The platform facilitates the broader use of AI-driven content production by allowing users to create and expand movies using straightforward instructions and graphics.

16-06-2026.title: How Are FTAs Driving Growth in India’s Electronics, Pharma and Engineering Exports?
16-06-2026.content:
Summary:

With electronics, pharmaceuticals, and engineering goods emerging as significant contributors, India's growing network of Free Trade Agreements (FTAs) is accelerating export growth. These industries have become more prominent in global markets thanks to preferential market access and reduced trade barriers, which indicate a move away from conventional commodity exports and toward more expensive manufactured and technologically advanced goods. FTAs have increased India's competitiveness, expanded its export markets, and strengthened its integration into international value chains. While electronics exports have increased quickly due to increased manufacturing capacity and worldwide demand, engineering goods continue to be one of the nation's top export categories. India's standing as a top provider of reasonably priced medications continues to boost pharmaceutical exports. Long-term economic development is anticipated to be supported by the expansion of these industries.

16-06-2026.title: How Did ESAF Small Finance Bank Cross the ₹50,000 Crore Business Milestone?
16-06-2026.content:
Summary:

As of June 11, 2026, ESAF Small Finance Bank had reached ₹50,029 crore, surpassing the ₹50,000 crore overall business milestone. A regulatory statement stated that gross advances were ₹23,832 crore and total deposits were ₹26,197 crore. The accomplishment is a result of the bank's consistent growth in the retail, microfinance, and rural banking sectors, which is bolstered by an expanding clientele and wide branch network. Along with bolstering its presence in underserved communities, the milestone also demonstrates balanced growth in deposits and lending. As a result of improved operations and asset quality, the bank reported a net profit of ₹24 crore for the quarter that ended in March 2026, as opposed to a net loss of ₹183 crore for the same period the previous year.

15-06-2026.title: How Is India Strengthening Its Biopharmaceutical Sector to Become a Global Leader by 2047?
15-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Through increased support for innovation, production, clinical research, and regulation, India is bolstering its biopharmaceutical industry. Union Minister Anupriya Patel emphasised the significance of biopharmaceuticals in the future of healthcare at the ASSOCHAM-organized 2nd Pharma Summit & Awards 2026. In line with the objectives of Viksit Bharat and Swasth Bharat, the Biopharma SHAKTI initiative seeks to introduce 100 biologics by 2047. Research, startups, and cutting-edge treatments are being supported by government programs including the PRIP Scheme, National Biopharma Mission, and BIRAC initiatives like BIG, SBIRI, and BIPP. Manoj Joshi, secretary of the Department of Pharmaceuticals, highlighted new capabilities, improved supply chains, and quicker drug discovery. Through the Pharma Excellence Awards, the summit also recognised accomplishments in the industry.

15-06-2026.title: How Has the Government Strengthened Farmer Welfare in the Last 12 Years?
15-06-2026.content:
Summary:

As the NDA celebrated 12 years in power, Prime Minister Narendra Modi emphasised the government's dedication to farmer welfare. Important programs including PM-KISAN, PMFBY, PM-KUSUM, and Kisan Credit Card have enhanced credit availability, financial inclusion, and income security. Almost 9.5 crore farmer families have received more than Rs. 4.28 lakh crore under PM-KISAN. Drones, Soil Health Cards, natural farming, and irrigation programs have all been used by the government to promote modern agriculture. About 26 crore Soil Health Cards have been issued since 2014–15, and PMFBY has resolved crop insurance claims of Rs. 1.92 lakh crore. Rice production exceeded 154 million tonnes in 2024–2025, while foodgrain production reached 358 million tonnes as a result of these efforts.

15-06-2026.title: How Are Free Trade Agreements and Global Partnerships Driving India’s Economic Transformation?
15-06-2026.content:
Summary:

At the India Global Innovation Connect summit, Union Minister Piyush Goyal emphasised India's approach to fortifying economic connections with developed countries through Free Trade Agreements (FTAs). FTAs, he said, encourage employment, commerce, investment, innovation, and integration into global value networks. Partner countries have pledged US$100 billion in investments and support for one million direct employment over a 15-year period under the Trade and Economic Partnership Agreement with EFTA countries—Switzerland, Norway, Liechtenstein, and Iceland. In recent years, India has expanded its market access and technological alliances by signing nine free trade agreements (FTAs) with 38 countries. India's standing as a competitive international investment and manufacturing destination is being further strengthened by ongoing reforms, infrastructural development, the expansion of renewable energy, and a Rs. 1 lakh crore R&D innovation fund.

15-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Prime Minister Narendra Modi highlighted the importance of cooperation, dialogue and knowledgesharing among states during the 11th Governing Council Meeting of NITI Aayog. He stated that cooperative federalism and coordinated efforts between the Centre and states are essential for achieving the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047. Despite global economic challenges, India continues to maintain strong growth momentum. The Prime Minister noted that expanding Free Trade Agreements are creating new opportunities for exports, investments and MSME growth. He also emphasised the need to harness India’s demographic dividend through investments in education, skill development and employment generation. The meeting underscored the growing role of women in development and reaffirmed the importance of collaborative policymaking, good governance and shared best practices for sustainable economic progress.

15-06-2026.title: Why Does Corporate India Remain Optimistic About Growth Prospects in FY27?
15-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Despite the volatility of commodity prices and geopolitical uncertainties, corporate India is still bullish about FY27 growth, according to an ICICI Securities analysis. While increased input costs are anticipated to be controlled through efficiency measures and gradual price increases, businesses are nonetheless optimistic about demand patterns and capital investment plans. In Q4FY26, the NSE500 universe had a 12% increase in profits, indicating widespread strength. Telecom, defence, metallurgy, building materials, power, banking, and consumer are important growth areas for FY27. While military continues to profit from robust order books, telecom is gaining from AI infrastructure and data center demand. Consumer demand is bolstered by premiumization, and banking continues to be robust with consistent credit growth. Real estate and hospitality are also exhibiting stronger growth momentum.

15-06-2026.title: How Is Indias Textile Industry Transforming into a Global Growth Engine by 2030?
15-06-2026.content:
Summary:

India’s textile industry has become a major growth driver, reaching about Rs. 16.66 lakh crore (US$ 190 billion) in 2025-26 and targeting Rs. 33.10 lakh crore (US$ 350 billion) by 2030. The domestic market has grown from Rs. 6 lakh crore in 2014-15 to over Rs. 16 lakh crore, driven by rising demand, stronger manufacturing and better value-chain integration. The sector provides employment to over 5.3 crore people and is expected to add around 2 crore more jobs in the coming years. Based on the 5F vision, it links farm to fibre, factory, fashion and foreign markets. Government support through PM MITRA Parks, PLI Scheme, NTTM, TEEM and other initiatives has strengthened competitiveness, exports and raw material supply, while FTAs and technical textiles growth are boosting global presence.

12-06-2026.title: How does a sitar concert in Riyadh strengthen India - Saudi cultural ties?
12-06-2026.content:
Summary:

"Desert Harmony Sitar Melodies," a cultural event highlighting Indian classical music and IndiaSaudi cultural ties, was organised by the Indian Embassy in Riyadh in collaboration with the Royal Commission for Riyadh City. During his performance, sitar master Ustad Shujaat Husain Khan showcased the richness of India's musical heritage. The event, which reflected the growing appreciation of Indian culture in Riyadh, attracted Saudi authorities, diplomats, members of the Indian community, and music enthusiasts. Dr. Suhel Ajaz Khan, the ambassador, emphasised the two countries' deep connections, common history, and growing cultural cooperation. Deeper participation and interpersonal relationships are facilitated via a Memorandum of Understanding on cultural collaboration. Through cultural diplomacy, the program improves bilateral ties in the areas of trade, energy, technology, and tourism. Cultural interactions open up new avenues for collaboration and development.

12-06-2026.title: How is India strengthening economic cooperation with Tajikistan through trade, investment, and technology partnerships?
12-06-2026.content:
Summary:

At the 12th Meeting of the India-Tajikistan Joint Commission on Trade, Economic, Scientific, and Technical Cooperation, which was conducted via video conference, India and Tajikistan reiterated their commitment to deepening bilateral economic ties. Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal emphasised the importance of using trade, investment, market access, and regulatory collaboration to transform great political goodwill into stronger economic benefits. The meeting, which was cochaired by representatives from both nations, examined new prospects for cooperation as well as the progress made since the last gathering. With 7.7% GDP growth and US$863 billion in total exports in FY26, India demonstrated its impressive economic success. Pharmaceuticals, healthcare, agriculture, energy, IT, and digital technology were recognised as important areas. India's exports to Tajikistan increased by 27.23%, with agricultural and pharmaceutical products leading the way. A protocol reaffirming a closer economic collaboration was signed by both parties.

Source: IBEF

12-06-2026.title: How will the India - Oman CEPA boost India’s exports and strengthen trade opportunities in the Gulf region?
12-06-2026.content:
Summary:

With effect from June 1, 2026, the India–Oman Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) gives India 100% duty-free access to 98.08% of Oman's tariff lines, which account for 99.38% of export value. Indian exports in labour-intensive industries including textiles, leather, marine products, jewellery and gems, plastics, sports goods, and agricultural products are anticipated to increase as a result of the agreement. India's competitiveness has increased thanks to Oman's instant duty-free entry for all textile and clothing categories and zero-duty access for handicrafts. The deal, which was signed by officials from both nations in December 2025, is regarded as a doorway to the larger GCC market. It is anticipated to improve trade facilitation, help MSMEs and craftspeople, and fortify supply chains. CEPA will increase India's export reach and diversity as bilateral commerce grows.

Source: IBEF

12-06-2026.title: How will the Viksit Bharat GRAMG initiative transform rural employment and village development across India?
12-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Under the "Viksit Bharat GRAMG" framework, the Center has announced a rural development project that would begin on July 1, 2026, with an allocation exceeding Rs. 1.25 lakh crore (US$13.09 billion). Through the smooth execution of rural projects and asset creation, the program seeks to improve village development and employment creation. Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan emphasised the importance of adopting a worker-centric strategy to safeguard rights, jobs, and salaries throughout the transition. Among the funds are Rs. 95,692.31 crore for temporary assistance to States and UTs and Rs. 30,000 crore under MGNREGA. The initiative, which covers about 2.8 lakh Gram Panchayats, supports digital governance systems like DBT and e-KYC, timely wage payments, and rural projects. It is a component of the larger Viksit Bharat 2047 vision for equitable rural development.

Source: IBEF

12-06-2026.title: How will Metas AI data centre in India impact the digital economy?
12-06-2026.content:
Summary:

In collaboration with Reliance Industries Ltd. (RIL), Facebook's parent company, META, has announced the opening of its first AI-enabled data center in India. With an initial capacity of 168 MW, the plant will be constructed near Jamnagar, Gujarat, and will significantly improve India's digital infrastructure. As per the arrangement, META would lease the center with options for future growth, while Reliance will develop and run it. The project draws attention to India's sizable internet user base and rapidly expanding AI ecosystem, which make it a desirable long-term investment location. Design, construction, energy integration, and connectivity will all be handled by Reliance. The data center, which is anticipated to be finished in two years, will handle digital apps, cloud services, and AI computing. It is expected to boost India's AI capabilities and the expansion of the digital economy.

Source: IBEF

12-06-2026.title: How will Hindustan Zinc’s partnership with Sulfozyme Agro improve sustainable metal recovery in mining operations?
12-06-2026.content:
Summary:

Sulfozyme Agro India Pvt. Ltd. and Hindustan Zinc Ltd. (HZL), the biggest integrated zinc producer in India, have inked a Memorandum of Understanding to create environmentally friendly and sustainable metal recovery solutions. In order to achieve the objectives of the circular economy and resource efficiency, the alliance will extract valuable metals from low-grade ores, mine waste, and tailings using bio-based and microbial technologies. It is consistent with Hindustan Zinc's emphasis on creative ways to enhance resource efficiency while minimising environmental effect. The project intends to boost metal recovery rates, cut waste, and encourage more environmentally friendly mining techniques by fusing biotechnology with mineral processing. Sulfozyme Agro will offer knowledge on bioprocessing techniques to extract metals from complicated materials. The partnership is anticipated to boost sustainability objectives, improve operational effectiveness, and fortify India's vital minerals value chain.

Source: IBEF

11-06-2026.title: How will India present its AI leadership at VivaTech 2026?
11-06-2026.content:
Summary:

As the Official AI Country Partner, India will make a significant technological statement at VivaTech 2026 in Paris, showcasing its advancements in digital innovation, artificial intelligence, and technology-led development. Through its largest national pavilion, the nation will highlight accomplishments in AI, digital public infrastructure, and large-scale innovation under the direction of ITPO. Under the theme "Tech for Humanity," which reflects inclusive, human-centered innovation, it will bring together government agencies, entrepreneurs, investors, and business leaders to forge alliances, draw in capital, and bolster international cooperation. The biggest tech festival in Europe, VivaTech, is set for June 17–20, 2026. With funding from DPIIT, MeitY, and the Ministry of Education India-France Year of Innovation 2026 project, India will showcase businesses in the fields of AI, SpaceTech, HealthTech, DeepTech, SaaS, ClimateTech, Robotics, and Cybersecurity.

Source: IBEF

11-06-2026.title: How has the Government e-Marketplace (GeM) transformed India’s public procurement ecosystem since 2016?
11-06-2026.content:
Summary:

The Government e-Marketplace (GeM), a transparent digital ecosystem, has revolutionised India's public procurement system since its introduction in 2016. It has made it possible for MSEs to acquire goods valued more than Rs. 8.69 lakh crore, increased the number of registered MSEs from 2,396 to over 11.9 lakh, and raised orders from 2,994 to more than 2.17 crore. By assisting womenowned businesses, startups, and SC/ST MSEs, it has increased inclusiveness while improving digital procurement procedures, efficiency, and transparency. Startup participation increased to over 40,000 organisations, SC/ST procurement surpassed Rs. 21,800 crore, and women-owned MSE procurement increased from Rs. 8 crore to over Rs. 93,327 crore. Additionally, the platform helped national efforts to purchase vaccines and syringes.

Source: IBEF

11-06-2026.title: How is India expanding digital inclusion and future-ready skilling through Solar Community Hub Skill Vans?
11-06-2026.content:
Summary:

To increase digital inclusion and future-ready skills, India has introduced five Solar Community Hub Skill Vans. The program, which was created by Dell Technologies in collaboration with NSDC and the Learning Links Foundation, provides underprivileged communities in India with technology-enabled education. The vans offer AI tools, internet, and vocational training in Uttar Pradesh, Delhi NCR, Karnataka, Telangana, and Maharashtra. With computers, screens, and renewable energy systems, they directly provide education to women, young people, and underprivileged communities. Basic literacy is replaced by tailored skill development that is in line with rising industries and labour market demands. Through inclusive growth and innovation, it supports more than fifty institutions and seeks to improve India's workforce prepared for the future. It has benefited 2.67 million people since FY23.

Source: IBEF

11-06-2026.title: How did APEDA facilitate Tezpur Litchi’s first export to Dubai?
11-06-2026.content:
Summary:

The one-metric-ton shipment, which was shipped on June 7, demonstrates the growing demand for India's GI-certified food around the world. Tezpur Litchi, which comes in types including Bombaya, Bilati, Elaichi, Piyaji, and Sahi, is renowned for its sweetness, red colour, aroma, and superior quality. Its export appeal and identity have been reinforced by the GI tag. Farmers have profited from the program by receiving prices that are almost 10% higher than those in nearby markets. In addition to increasing export prospects for Northeastern agricultural products abroad, APEDA's certification, infrastructure, training, and market linkage initiatives are assisting farmers in becoming integrated into global value chains.

11-06-2026.title: How is Indias flexi-staffing industry driving formal employment growth in FY26 despite global challenges?
11-06-2026.content:
Summary:

According to the Indian Staffing Federation (ISF), the country's flexi-staffing sector saw an 8% increase in new jobs year over year in FY26. With the addition of 1.18 lakh new formal flexi workers in the last 12 months, the total number of ISF members is now 1.91 million. This growth demonstrated the durability of India's personnel ecosystem in the face of economic challenges and worldwide geopolitical unpredictability in 2025 and early 2026. According to the survey, more workers have entered structured flexi-staffing roles as a result of increased formalisation of employment, which has improved job access for recent graduates and first-time job searchers. Additionally, the sector promotes agile talent solutions for companies and more stable economic conditions. Growth was further accelerated in FY26 by a high demand for IT personnel.
